LEVELONE_EQUITIES.fieldDescription: Price at which the last trade was matched

LEVELONE_EQUITIES.notesAndExamples: Units are "lots" (typically 100 shares per lot). Note for NFL data this field can be 0 with a non-zero bid price which representing a bid size of less than 100 shares.
See bid size notes.

LEVELONE_EQUITIES.fieldDescription: Aggregated shares traded throughout the day, including pre/post market hours.
LEVELONE_EQUITIES.notesAndExamples: Volume is set to zero at 7:28am ET.

LEVELONE_EQUITIES.fieldDescription: Number of shares traded with last trade
LEVELONE_EQUITIES.notesAndExamples: Units are shares
According to industry standard, only regular session trades set the High and Low. If a stock does not trade in the regular session, high and low will be zero. High/low reset to ZERO at 3:30am ET.
See High Price notes
Closing prices are updated from the DB at 3:30 AM ET.

As long as the symbol is valid, this data is always present. This field is updated every time the closing prices are loaded from DB. Exchange Mapping: AMEX=A (Both), Indices=0 (Realtime Only), Mutual Fund=3 (Realtime Only), NASDAQ=Q (Both), NYSE=N (Both), Pacific=P (Both), Pinks=9 (Realtime Only), OTCBB=U (Realtime Only)

LEVELONE_EQUITIES.fieldDescription: Stock approved by the Federal Reserve and an investor's broker as being eligible for providing collateral for margin debt.
A company, index or fund name
LEVELONE_EQUITIES.notesAndExamples: Once per day descriptions are loaded from the database at 7:29:50 AM ET.

LEVELONE_EQUITIES.fieldDescription: Exchange where last trade was executed

LEVELONE_EQUITIES.notesAndExamples: According to industry standard, only regular session trades set the open. If a stock does not trade during the regular session, then the open price is 0. In the pre-market session, open is blank because pre-market session trades do not set the open. Open is set to ZERO at 3:30am ET.
If close is zero, change will be zero

LEVELONE_EQUITIES.fieldDescription: Highest price traded in the past 12 months, or 52 weeks
LEVELONE_EQUITIES.notesAndExamples: Calculated by merging intraday high (from fh) and 52-week high (from db)

LEVELONE_EQUITIES.fieldDescription: Lowest price traded in the past 12 months, or 52 weeks
LEVELONE_EQUITIES.notesAndExamples: Calculated by merging intraday low (from fh) and 52-week low (from db)

LEVELONE_EQUITIES.fieldDescription: Price-to-earnings ratio. The P/E equals the price of a share of stock, divided by the company's earnings-per-share.
LEVELONE_EQUITIES.notesAndExamples: Note that the "price of a share of stock" in the definition does update during the day so this field has the potential to stream. However, the current implementation uses the closing price and therefore does not stream throughout the day.
Load various times after market close

LEVELONE_EQUITIES.fieldDescription: Is last quote a regular quote
Is last trade a regular trade
Currently realize/100, only records regular trade
Indicates a symbols current trading status, Normal, Halted, Closed
Last time a bid or ask updated in milliseconds since Epoch
LEVELONE_EQUITIES.notesAndExamples: The difference, measured in milliseconds, between the time an event occurs and midnight, January 1, 1970 UTC.

LEVELONE_EQUITIES.fieldDescription: Last trade time in milliseconds since Epoch
LEVELONE_EQUITIES.notesAndExamples: The difference, measured in milliseconds, between the time an event occurs and midnight, January 1, 1970 UTC.

LEVELONE_EQUITIES.fieldName: Regular Market Trade Time in Long
LEVELONE_EQUITIES.fieldDescription: Regular market trade time in milliseconds since Epoch
LEVELONE_EQUITIES.notesAndExamples: The difference, measured in milliseconds, between the time an event occurs and midnight, January 1, 1970 UTC.

LEVELONE_EQUITIES.fieldDescription: Last bid time in milliseconds since Epoch
LEVELONE_EQUITIES.notesAndExamples: The difference, measured in milliseconds, between the time an event occurs and midnight, January 1, 1970 UTC.

LEVELONE_EQUITIES.fieldDescription: Last ask time in milliseconds since Epoch
LEVELONE_EQUITIES.notesAndExamples: The difference, measured in milliseconds, between the time an event occurs and midnight, January 1, 1970 UTC.
NetChange / ClosePrice * 100
RegularMarketNetChange / ClosePrice * 100
-1 = NULL, >= 0 is valid quantity
null = NULL, valid range = -99,999.999 to +99,999.999
-1 = NULL, 1 = true, 0 = false
-1 = NULL, 1 = true, 0 = false

LEVELONE_EQUITIES.fieldDescription: Change in price since the end of the regular session (typically 4:00pm)
LEVELONE_EQUITIES.notesAndExamples: PostMarketLastPrice - RegularMarketLastPrice

LEVELONE_EQUITIES.fieldDescription: Percent Change in price since the end of the regular session (typically 4:00pm)
LEVELONE_EQUITIES.notesAndExamples: PostMarketNetChange / RegularMarketLastPrice * 100

LEVELONE_OPTIONS.fieldDescription: A company, index or fund name
LEVELONE_OPTIONS.notesAndExamples: Descriptions are loaded from the database daily at 3:30 am ET.

LEVELONE_OPTIONS.fieldDescription: Price at which the last trade was matched

LEVELONE_OPTIONS.notesAndExamples: According to industry standard, only regular session trades set the High and Low. If a stock does not trade in the regular session, high and low will be zero. High/low reset to zero at 3:30am ET.
Closing prices are updated from the DB at 7:29AM ET.

LEVELONE_OPTIONS.fieldDescription: Aggregated contracts traded throughout the day, including pre/post market hours.
LEVELONE_OPTIONS.notesAndExamples: Volume is set to zero at 3:30am ET.
Volatility is reset to 0 at 3:30am ET.

LEVELONE_OPTIONS.fieldDescription: The value an option would have if it were exercised today. Basically, the intrinsic value is the amount by which the strike price of an option is profitable or in-the-money as compared to the underlying stock's price in the market.
LEVELONE_OPTIONS.notesAndExamples: In-the-money is positive, out-of-the money is negative.

According to industry standard, only regular session trades set the open. If a stock does not trade during the regular session, then the open price is 0. In the pre-market session, open is blank because pre-market session trades do not set the open. Open is set to ZERO at 7:28 ET.

LEVELONE_OPTIONS.fieldDescription: Number of contracts traded with last trade

LEVELONE_OPTIONS.notesAndExamples: If(close>0) change = last – close Else change=0
Indicates a symbol's current trading status: Normal, Halted, Closed

LEVELONE_OPTIONS.fieldDescription: Last quote time in milliseconds since Epoch
LEVELONE_OPTIONS.notesAndExamples: The difference, measured in milliseconds, between the time an event occurs and midnight, January 1, 1970 UTC.

LEVELONE_OPTIONS.fieldDescription: Last trade time in milliseconds since Epoch
LEVELONE_OPTIONS.notesAndExamples: The difference, measured in milliseconds, between the time an event occurs and midnight, January 1, 1970 UTC.
Only valid for index options (0 for all other options)
Only valid for index options (0 for all other options)

LEVELONE_OPTIONS.fieldDescription: The latest time the indicative bid/ask prices updated in milliseconds since Epoch
LEVELONE_OPTIONS.notesAndExamples: Only valid for index options (0 for all other options). The difference, measured in milliseconds, between the time an event occurs and midnight, January 1, 1970 UTC.

LEVELONE_FUTURES.fieldDescription: Price at which the last trade was matched

LEVELONE_FUTURES.notesAndExamples: Currently "?" for unknown as all quotes are CME
Currently "?" for unknown as all quotes are CME

LEVELONE_FUTURES.fieldDescription: Aggregated contracts traded throughout the day, including pre/post market hours.
Number of contracts traded with last trade
Time of the last quote in milliseconds since epoch
Time of the last trade in milliseconds since epoch

LEVELONE_FUTURES.notesAndExamples: Currently "?" for unknown as all quotes are CME

LEVELONE_FUTURES.fieldDescription: Exchange where last trade was executed

LEVELONE_FUTURES.notesAndExamples: If(close>0) change = last – close else change=0
Indicates a symbols current trading status, Normal, Halted, Closed

LEVELONE_FUTURES.fieldDescription: The total number of futures contracts that are not closed or delivered on a particular day
Mark-to-Market value is calculated daily using current prices to determine profit/loss
LEVELONE_FUTURES.notesAndExamples: If lastprice is within spread, value = lastprice else value=(bid+ask)/2

LEVELONE_FUTURES.fieldDescription: Minimum amount that the price of the market can change
Display in fraction or decimal format.
LEVELONE_FUTURES.notesAndExamples: Set from FSP Config format is , where D=decimal format, no fractional display. Equity futures will be "D,D" to indicate pure decimal. Fixed income futures are fractional, typically "3,32". Below is an example for "3,32": price=101.8203125 =101 + 0.8203125 (split into whole and fractional) =101 + 26.25/32 (Multiply fractional by implied denomiator) =101 + 26.2/32 (round to numerator decimals to display) =101'262 (display in fractional format)

days: 0 = monday-friday, 1 = sunday, 7 = Saturday. 0 = [-2000,1700] ==> open, close. 1= [-1530,-1630,-1700,1515] ==> open, close, open, close. 0 = [-1800,1700,d, 1700,1900] ==> open, close, DST-flag, open, close

LEVELONE_FUTURES.fieldDescription: Flag to indicate if this future contract is tradable
Indicates if this contract is active
Time of the last ask-side quote in milliseconds since epoch
Time of the last bid-side quote in milliseconds since epoch
Indicates if this contract has quoted during the active session
